name: Atayal
notes: STUB: the grapheme inventory of Atayal is NOT yet specified (graphemes/allophones are empty). Atayal (Tayal) is a Formosan (Austronesian) language of northern Taiwan, written in the Latin script under the Council of Indigenous Peoples' 2005 romanisation. Dialects (Squliq, Cʼuliʼ/Matuʼuwal) differ phonologically, so a single letter→IPA table would need to be dialect-scoped. INPUT CONTRACT: Latin-script (official romanisation) Atayal text. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode atay1247), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode atay1247.

ancestors.notes: Austronesian — the classification node this language sits under.
